reachability_artifacts: built sworn maintainability review run from cmd/sworn/maintainability_git_boundary_test.go:TestMaintainabilityGitBoundaryBinaryReachability reaches the committed-Git boundary and then fails closed at the next unavailable gate

effort_complexity.rationale: Broad but deterministic Git plumbing surface proven against real repositories.

release_benefit: Gives every later semantic and integration authority one binary-safe committed-object foundation.

design_decisions.choice: Introduce the final maintainability command as a fail-closed scaffold at the committed-Git boundary and extend it serially in T2.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 Coach instruction to follow the orchestrator recommendation: use the serial final-command scaffold.
design_decisions.rationale: The production entry point reaches each authority as it lands without granting later mutation or dispatch authority early.

positive_scenarios: Adversarial committed paths and blobs retain exact identities through typed APIs.
negative_scenarios: Unsafe input, missing history, conflict, dirtiness, or exact-ref mismatch fails without leaking payloads.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Raw object correctness prevents higher-level Baton rules from depending on lossy human-oriented Git output.
